title: 6 Interac e-TransferÂ® Banking Host Remediation Process
body_text:
6	Interac e-Transfer
Â®
Banking Host Remediation Process
6.1	Overview of the Remediation Process
Interac
e-Transfer transactions or cancellations can fail due to time-out errors which can occur between Central 1 and your banking host when theyâre exchanging information with each other. To combat these errors, Central 1 employs a remediation process for sending, receiving, and cancelling Interac e-Transfer transactions that uses a fail-safe mechanism to avoid loss of funds and to clean up any previous failed transaction activity. As per ISO 20022 and ISO 8583 specifications, your banking host should never reject Central 1âs reversal messages. Your banking host will either locate the original commit message to proceed with the reversal message or it wonât. Regardless, your banking host responds to them with response codes â000â (successful) or â400â (reversal accepted). The remediation process runs every 15 minutes until the cut-off time (12:00 am ET) or until a set number of reversal attempts have been processed. The latter is dependent on what error response Central 1 receives from your banking host. If the transaction isnât remediated before the cut-off time, the transaction will be included in your daily transaction reports for manual reconciliation.
Important
:	Your banking host should never reject any confirm or commit credit messages since Central 1 has already completed the transaction with Interac.
6.2	Errors when Receiving (Crediting) Interac e-Transfer Transactions
If your financial institution has Interac 3.4 deposits, the initiate or proposal message sent for these deposits is approved by your banking host. If your banking host rejects the confirm or credit message for any reason, Central 1 begins the remediation process to ensure the funds are accepted by your banking host before the cut-off time on the day it occurs. All credit remediation processes begin with reversing any previous commit credit messages followed by a re-commit credit message (assuming the reversal is accepted by your banking host to avoid double posting).
There are three common scenarios you may experience when receiving Interac e-Transfer transactions:
Scenario 1
â
Your Banking Host Doesnât Process Central 1âs Confirm or Commit Credit Message
Central 1 sends out a confirm or credit message to your banking host.
Your banking host doesnât process the request (funds werenât credited to the recipientâs account).
The banking host confirms that the confirm or commit credit message time-out occurred.
Central 1 begins the remediation process and sends out a credit reversal message.
Your banking host doesnât find the original transaction to reverse and responds to the credit reversal message.
Central 1 responds to the confirm credit message.
The banking host credits the account once the confirm credit message is processed. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Banking Host Doesnât Process the Confirm or Commit Credit Message
Scenario 2
â
Your Banking Host Processes Central 1âs Confirm or Commit Credit Message
Central 1 sends the confirm or commit credit message to the banking host.
The banking host receives the message and processes the request (funds are credited to the recipientâs account).
The request is confirmed, but it encounters a time-out error.
Central 1 begins the remediation process and sends a credit reversal message.
Your banking host responds to the credit reversal message.
Central 1 sends the confirm or commit credit message to the banking host again.
Your banking host responds to the confirm or credit message and credits the recipientâs account. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Banking Host Processes the Confirm or Commit Credit Message
Scenario 3
â
Your Banking Host Rejects the Confirm or Commit Credit Message
Central 1 sends the confirm or commit credit message to your banking host.
Your banking host rejects the confirm or commit credit message.
Central 1 begins the remediation process and sends a credit reversal request.
Your banking host responds to the reversal message.
Central 1 sends the confirm or commit credit message again.
Your banking host responds to the confirm or credit message and credits the recipientâs account. See Figure 3.
Note:	If the process encounters any failures by the end of day cut-off time, the transaction will appear on the ECCX report. This will be settled on the next settlement date. See Section 8.4, ECCX (e-Transfer Failed Transactions Report), for more information on the report.
Figure 3: Banking Host Rejects the Confirm or Commit Credit Message
6.3	Errors When Sending (Debiting) Interac e-Transfer Transactions
Central 1 sends debit messages to banking hosts when a customer is sending a transaction or when theyâre fulfilling a Money Request. When the sender accepts the initiate or propose debit request, Central 1 sends the debit commit message to your banking host. Once the debit is deemed successful, Central 1 sends a message to Interac. If your banking hosts rejects the debit commit message, there is nothing to action as no account outages have occurred. However, if a time-out error occurs, Central 1 doesnât know if funds have been debited from the senderâs account. At this point, Central 1 begins the remediation process and sends a reversal message to correct the previous debit.
Common scenarios that may occur when sending transactions are:
Scenario 1
â
Your Banking Host Doesnât Process Central 1âs Confirm or Commit Debit Message
Central 1 sends a commit debit message to your banking host.
Your banking host doesnât process the request (funds havenât been removed from the senderâs account).
A debit time-out is confirmed.
Central 1 begins the remediation process and sends a debit reversal message to your banking host.
Your banking host is unable to locate the original debit message to reverse. They respond to the reversal message.
Central 1 confirms the transaction is successfully remediated. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Banking Host Doesnât Process the Confirm or Commit Debit Message
Scenario 2
â
Your Banking Host Processes Central 1âs Confirm or Commit Debit Message
Central 1 sends a confirm or commit debit message to your banking host.
Your banking host receives the message and processes the request (funds are debited from the senderâs account).
A debit time-out is confirmed.
Central 1 begins the remediation process and sends a debit reversal message to your banking host.
Your banking host locates the transaction to reverse and successfully responds to the reversal message after performing the debit reversal on the senderâs account.
Central 1 confirms the transaction is successfully remediated. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Banking Host Processes the Confirm or Commit Debit Message
6.4	Errors When Cancelling a Pending Interac e-Transfer Transaction
Customers from sending financial institutions can cancel a pending transaction, but there is a possibility an error can occur while the request is being processed.
Scenario â The Customer Cancels a Pending Interac e-Transfer Transaction, but itâs Unsuccessful
Central 1 cancels the transaction with Interac.
Central 1 sends a cancel or reverse-debit message to reverse the debit from the senderâs account.
Your banking host doesnât respond to the cancel or reverse-debit message (no funds are credited back to the senderâs account).
A debit time-out occurs.
Central 1 begins the remediation process to reprocess the reversal request. The cancel or reverse-debit message is sent again.
Your banking host successfully responds to this message and funds are credited into the senderâs account. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Pending Interac e-Transfer Cancellation Encounters an Error
END
